name: William McKinley
title: Last known photo of President William McKinley, captured at the Pan-American Exposition in Buffalo on September 6, 1901, minutes before he was shot inside the Temple of Music.
description: This is the last known photograph of President William McKinley, taken on September 6, 1901, as he walked toward the Temple of Music at the Pan-American Exposition in Buffalo, New York. Inside, a few minutes later, McKinley stood in a public reception line shaking hands with attendees. An anarchist named Leon Czolgosz, with a revolver concealed under a handkerchief on his right hand, fired twice at point-blank range. One bullet glanced off a button; the other tore through his stomach, pancreas, and kidney. McKinley initially seemed to be recovering after surgery, but gangrene set in around the wound. He died at 2:15 a.m. on September 14, 1901, eight days after the shooting. Czolgosz was tried, convicted, and executed by electric chair in October 1901. McKinley was 58 years old. The Temple of Music was demolished in 1903; today a granite marker (the “McKinley Shooting Rock”) sits on the spot. At least ten Buffalo and Niagara photographers later claimed to have taken the day's final image of the President; this arrival shot by James H. “Jimmy” Hare of Collier's Weekly received the most authoritative recognition, credited as “the last photograph of the living President” by the British Journal of Photography in 1904.
bio: William McKinley was the 25th president of the United States, serving from 1897 until his assassination. A Republican from Ohio and the last president to have served in the American Civil War, he led the country through the Spanish-American War, oversaw the annexation of Hawaii, Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines, and advanced the gold standard and protective tariffs that defined the era. He was the third U.S. president to be assassinated, after Lincoln and Garfield.

location.name: McKinley Shooting Rock (site of the Temple of Music), 30 Fordham Dr, Buffalo, NY 14216
